Learning Python From JavaScript (30): Building a Web Scraper

We've picked up a fair amount of Python by now, and this is the last day of the Ironman contest — so let's put it all together and build a simple scraper for the iThome Ironman site. Using Requests plus BeautifulSoup, we'll grab the topic list, page through each topic's contestants, and save everything out to JSON.
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (28) - Closures
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle

JavaScript has this thing called a closure. Does Python have it too? In this chapter we'll have a relaxed chat about how to write a closure in Python if you want one — including the one syntax trap that'll throw a SyntaxError if you try to copy JavaScript too literally.
Learn Python From a JS Angle (17): Version Management and Virtual Environments
Learn Python From a JS Angle

Last time we covered the basic pip commands, but I left a couple of problems hanging without explaining how to fix them. So this post is all about the fix: using venv to isolate your project's packages (think package.json), and using pyenv to switch Python versions the same way nvm switches Node.js versions.
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (6) - Variable Scope

Last time we covered functions and variables, so now I want to circle back and fill in something I skipped: variable scope. We'll look at how Python's scoping differs from JavaScript's var/let/const, why Python behaves a lot like var, whether Python has a scope chain, and what nonlocal and global are actually for.
